TORNADO RATED EF1. THE TORNADO BEGAN AROUND 3.4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MARQUAND AND CROSSED INTO BOLLINGER COUNTY AROUND 1.4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF MARQUAND. THE PATH LENGTH IN MADISON COUNTY WAS AROUND 4.4 MILES AND MAX PATH WIDTH AROUND 0.3 MILES.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
VIA FACEBOOK REPORT WITH PICTURE.

DELAYED REPORT. LOCATION AND TIME BOTH APPROXIMATE AND ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. PHOTOS POSTED TO SOCIAL MEDIA OF TREE DAMAGE, STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A HOME, AND CAR DAMAGE NEAR THE TOWN OF BUTTS AND THE HUZZAH CREEK RECREATIONAL AREA. TWO GRAIN BINS WERE SWEPT OFF OF THEIR CONCRETE PADS. TWO OUTBUILDINGS WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED. MULTIPLE LARGE MATURE TREES WERE UPROOTED. A PROPANE TANK WAS ALSO OVERTURNED.

TORANDO RATED EF0. PATH LENGTH OF 5.75 MILES AND MAX PATH WIDTH AROUND 100 YARDS. THE TORNADO BEGAN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE HUZZAH RIVER APPROXIMATELY 8 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF STEELVILLE WHERE A BARN WAS DAMAGED. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED GENERALLY EASTWARD HITTING A FARMSTEAD AT THE END OF MULLEN ROAD. MOST OF THE DAMAGE CONSISTED OF DOWNED TREES AND BROKEN BRANCHES. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND TWO SMALL GRAIN BINS WERE DESTROYED, ALONG WITH SUPERFICIAL DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES. THE REMAINDER OF DAMAGE CONSISTED OF DOWNED TREES AND BRANCHES AS THE TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD THROUGH THE NORTHERN EDGE OF BUTTS, JUST MISSING A LARGE OUTDOOR OUTFITTER AND RECREATIONAL AREA ON THE COURTOIS CREEK. THE TORNADO ENDED WITHIN A VALLEY EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF THUNDER VALLEY ROAD AND CHRISTY MINE ROAD, LESS THAN ONE MILE FROM THE CRAWFORD/WASHINGTON COUNTY LINE.
FROM FACEBOOK POST WITH PICTURE. TIME ESTIMATED.

SPOTTER REPORTED THAT SEVERAL HOUSES HAVE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, SOME COLLAPSED. THE SCHOOL HAS SUFFERED EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE. SEVERAL TREES ARE STRIPPED AND POWER LINES ARE DOWN IN THE AREA. ROADS ARE IMPASSABLE IN MANY AREAS WITH DEBRIS THROUGHOUT THE EASTERN SIDE OF TOWN.

EXTENSIVE DAMAGE STARTS AROUND CHRISTY DRIVE AND HIGHWAY 179 AND TRACKS TO THE NORTHEAST. DAMAGE PATH CROSSES ELLIS BLVD NEAR HIGHWAY 54 AND CONTINUED ON NEAR TANNER BRIDGE. THE 500 BLOCK OF ELLIS BLVD AND APPROXIMATE AREA AROUND THE 600 BLOCK OF STADIUM BLVD HAS SUFFERED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE, WHICH INCLUDES JEFFERSON HIGH SCHOOL. AT THIS TIME THERE IS NO CREDIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE MISSOURI TROOP HQ HAS BEEN IMPACTED. THE SOUTHEAST SIDE OF TOWN IS COMPLETELY DARK DUE TO POWER OUTAGES.

SOME OF THE TREES WERE LARGE TREES. POWER LINES WERE DOWN WITH FEW POWER POLES BROKEN ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE SUBDIVISION. A WEAK TORNADO WAS OBSERVED AND PHOTOGRAPHED BY SEVERAL INDIVIDUALS INCLUDING A STORM CHASER AND SEVERE STORM SPOTTER. THE TORNADO TRACKED WEST TO EAST ACROSS THE NORTH PORTION OF BENLD PRODUCING DAMAGE TO TREES AND POWER LINES AND MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES. THE TORNADO LASTED APPROXIMATELY 4 MINUTES AND WAS RATED EF0 WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.83 MILES AND MAX WIDTH AROUND 30 YARDS.

GRAVOIS CREEK ROSE ABOVE ITS BANKS FLOODING SEVERAL LOCATIONS INCLUDING A PARKING LOT AT WHITECLIFF PARK. TWO VEHICLES WERE MOVED FURTHER DOWN THE LOT BY THE FLOOD WATERS AND ONE PERSON HAD TO BE RESCUED FROM THE FLOWING WATER IN THAT AREA. PARTS OF PARDEE AND GRANT ROADS WERE BRIEFLY FLOODED AS WELL.
